page: Amazon Web Services c7gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and is equipped with 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network performance is rated at an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory write performance, with large block uncached write bandwidth reaching 96.91 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems. The Passmark memory score also ranks in the top 25%. However, CPU encryption performance is relatively weak, scoring 2232.77 in the Passmark encryption test. Multi-core processing capabilities are demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 24502.63 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13042.0. In database and web serving workloads, the instance achieves 26.78 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 29.03 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

The c7gd.4xlarge offers a balanced cost-to-performance profile for ARM-native workloads, combining dedicated compute resources with high-speed local NVMe storage. The inclusion of local SSD storage eliminates the need for external block storage for temporary data, enhancing resource density and cost efficiency. This instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads that require fast local scratch space, such as distributed databases, high-performance web serving, application servers, and software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM-based instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark results highlight strong memory write bandwidth at 96.91 GB/sec and a high Passmark memory score, though CPU encryption performance is comparatively weak. The instance delivers solid multi-core performance with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13042.0 and handles 26.78 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. By bundling high-speed local storage with dedicated ARM compute resources, the c7gd.4xlarge provides a cost-efficient platform for workloads requiring fast local scratch space, such as distributed databases, web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.4xlarge is an ARM-based compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 950 GB local NVMe SSD storage, ideal for databases and web serving.

tagline: An ARM-powered compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM cores at 2.6 GHz
Includes 32.0 GB of system memory and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong memory write performance of 96.91 GB/sec in large block tests
Features 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Well-suited for distributed databases, web serving, and software compilation
